response:
The first phase needs ten hours of work.  
The second phase takes twenty hours to finish.  
The third phase requires forty hours of effort.  
The fourth phase demands eighty hours of time.  
The fifth phase uses one hundred sixty hours.  
All phases sum to three hundred ten hours total.  
Each phase now lasts one point five times longer.  
The original plan spanned twenty weeks in all.  
New duration equals thirty weeks for the whole.  
No change occurs in the order of tasks.  
Teams must adapt to this shift in schedule.  
Resources will be stretched by the delay.  
The manager will review the updated timeline soon.  
Fortune favors those who plan with care and skill.
